title: Remove A Faulted Motionless AGV From The Floor And Restore Operation
summary: Recover from an AGV motionless-on-floor fault by locating the faulted AGV, identifying and stopping the correct E-stop zone, removing the AGV from the floor, then restoring operation with the documented E-stop release and blue RESET sequence.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV is faulted and motionless on the floor and the documented troubleshooting sequence calls for locating the AGV, stopping the appropriate E-stop zone, removing the AGV, and resetting the system.
not_for: Do not use when the faulted AGV cannot be identified from floor observation and RMS reference.
Do not use as a detailed physical AGV removal method; the source does not describe how to physically remove the AGV.
Do not continue this procedure if the AGV cannot be safely removed or if the system does not resume operation after the documented reset sequence.

responsibilities: Reference RMS for AGV error information if needed.
Identify the correct E-stop zone from the zone map.
Remove the faulted AGV from the floor using site-approved safe handling methods.
Release the E-stop and press the blue RESET button.

safety_notes: This procedure is not marked support-safe because the source does not describe the safe handling method for physically removing the AGV.
Use site safety controls and local procedures for physical AGV removal; this source does not provide those details.
Stop and escalate if the AGV cannot be safely removed.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to RMS for AGV error reference
Access to the E-stop zone map
Access to the E-STOP control for the appropriate zone
Access to the blue RESET button
Physical access to the faulted AGV

access_required: Access to RMS for AGV error reference
Access to the E-stop zone map
Access to the E-STOP control for the appropriate zone
Access to the blue RESET button
Physical access to the faulted AGV
system_preconditions: A faulted AGV is motionless on the floor.
The appropriate E-stop zone can be identified before stopping the zone.

visual_references.description: Screen showing a faulted AGV with a tote during fault recovery; included as candidate image support for identifying a faulted AGV context.
System screen artifact associated with ESTOP clearing and RESET context.

steps.title: Locate the faulted AGV
steps.purpose: Identify which AGV is faulted and motionless so the correct recovery actions can be applied.
steps.instruction: Locate the faulted AGV on the floor. If needed, reference RMS for AGV error information to help identify the AGV.
steps.expected_result: The faulted motionless AGV is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The specific AGV requiring recovery is clearly identified from floor observation and/or RMS error information.
steps.failure_condition: The faulted AGV cannot be confidently identified.

actions.label: Reference RMS error information
actions.exact_action: May need to reference RMS for AGV error.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as a visual example of a faulted AGV screen context during recovery.

stop_or_escalate_if: The faulted AGV cannot be identified from floor observation and RMS reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: AGV motionless on floor Fault... 1. Locate the faulted AGV (may need to reference RMS for AGV error).
In the AGV motionless on floor troubleshooting entry, RMS is referenced as a source for AGV error information to help locate the faulted AGV.

steps.title: Identify the appropriate E-stop zone
steps.purpose: Ensure the correct zone is isolated before AGV removal.
steps.instruction: Reference the E-stop zone map to identify the appropriate E-stop zone for the faulted AGV.
steps.expected_result: The correct E-stop zone is identified.
steps.healthy_condition: The E-stop zone corresponding to the faulted AGV location is known before the zone is stopped.
steps.failure_condition: The correct E-stop zone cannot be determined.
stop_or_escalate_if: The correct E-stop zone cannot be determined.

steps.title: Stop the appropriate E-stop zone
steps.purpose: Isolate the area so the faulted AGV can be removed.
steps.instruction: Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.
steps.expected_result: The appropriate E-stop zone is stopped.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended E-stop zone is stopped and the area is isolated for AGV removal.
steps.failure_condition: The zone does not stop as expected or the wrong zone is affected.

actions.label: Press E-STOP
actions.exact_action: Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.

stop_or_escalate_if: The zone does not stop as expected.
There is uncertainty that the correct zone was stopped.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: 2. Reference E-stop zone map. Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone.

steps.title: Remove the faulted AGV from the floor
steps.purpose: Clear the faulted AGV from the floor so operation can be restored.
steps.instruction: Remove the faulted AGV from the floor.
steps.expected_result: The faulted AGV is removed from the floor.
steps.healthy_condition: The faulted AGV is no longer on the floor in the affected area.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV cannot be removed or cannot be safely removed.
stop_or_escalate_if: The AGV cannot be safely removed.
The source does not provide the physical removal method and site-approved handling guidance is unavailable.

steps.title: Release the E-stop
steps.purpose: Prepare the system for reset after AGV removal.
steps.healthy_condition: The E-stop is pulled out/released and the system is ready for reset.
steps.failure_condition: The E-stop cannot be released or remains in a faulted state.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference ESTOP clearing and RESET context associated with pulling out ESTOP and clearing faults.

stop_or_escalate_if: The E-stop remains faulted after release.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: At the operator station control panel, if the ESTOP is flashing, pull out the ESTOP and press RESET on the HMI screen to clear any faults.

steps.title: Press the blue RESET button
steps.purpose: Clear the fault condition and restore the system after E-stop release.
steps.expected_result: The reset is accepted and the system clears the fault condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The reset completes without additional fault indication.
steps.failure_condition: The reset does not clear the fault or the system remains stopped.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Candidate-provided image support for a faulted AGV screen during the fault recovery procedure.
Reference RESET-related HMI context associated with clearing faults after ESTOP is pulled out.

stop_or_escalate_if: The reset does not clear the fault.
The system remains stopped after reset.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The manual references a flashing ESTOP condition at the operator station control panel and states that RESET on the HMI screen is used to clear faults after the ESTOP is pulled out.

steps.title: Verify the system resumes operation
steps.purpose: Confirm the documented recovery sequence succeeded.
steps.instruction: Verify that the system resumes operation.
steps.healthy_condition: Normal operation resumes after the reset sequence.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not resume operation.
stop_or_escalate_if: The system does not resume operation after the documented reset sequence.

success_criteria: The appropriate E-stop zone is stopped.
The faulted AGV is removed from the floor.
The E-stop is released and the blue RESET button is pressed.
healthy_conditions: The faulted AGV can be identified using floor observation and, if needed, RMS error information.
The correct E-stop zone can be identified from the zone map.
The appropriate zone stops when E-STOP is pressed.
The AGV is successfully removed from the floor.
The reset sequence completes and the system resumes operation.
failure_conditions: The faulted AGV cannot be identified from floor observation and RMS reference.
The correct E-stop zone cannot be determined.
The AGV cannot be safely removed.
The reset sequence does not clear the fault.
The system does not resume operation after the documented reset sequence.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the faulted AGV cannot be identified from floor observation and RMS reference.
Escalate if the AGV cannot be safely removed.
Escalate if the system does not resume operation after the documented reset sequence.
Escalate for site safety review because the source does not describe how to physically remove the AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Faulted AGV screen context referenced by the candidate for recovery image support.
ESTOP and RESET-related HMI context for clearing faults after ESTOP release.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: AGV motionless on floor Fault... 1. Locate the faulted AGV (may need to reference RMS for AGV error). 2. Reference E-stop zone map. Press E-STOP to stop the appropriate E-stop zone. 3. Remove the faulted AGV. 4. Pull E-STOP. 5. Press the blue RESET button. 6. The system will resume operation.

missing_details: The source does not describe the physical method, tools, or safety controls for removing the faulted AGV from the floor.
The source does not provide a time estimate.
The source does not specify whether production stop is required beyond stopping the appropriate E-stop zone.
The source does not specify whether LOTO is required.
The source does not identify the exact location or interface of the blue RESET button for this procedure.
